preamble: The shift from the voiced palatal nasal [ɲ] to the voiced velar nasal [ŋ] is a process of retraction or backing. This transformation typically occurs as a result of regressive place assimilation when a palatal nasal precedes a velar consonant, or as part of a systemic nasal harmony process.

examples.note: The moraic nasal /n/ exhibits place assimilation, retracting from palatal [ɲ] to velar [ŋ] when followed by [k] or [ɡ] (e.g., 'kongo' [koŋɡo]).
Systemic nasal harmony can pull a palatal nasal back to a velar position in specific phonetic environments to maintain word-level harmony.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
